pages.raw_text:
This morning a change of venue
was taken from Judge Mann's court
in the case of the five members of
the Industrial Workers arrested
last night at the 1. w. w. hall on a
charge of criminal conspiracy.


Mrs. J. A. Jones, known among
socialists and in the Industrial
movement by her maiden name,
Elizabeth Gurley Flynn, was among
those arrested. She was taken into
custody as she was approaching the
l. w. w. hall. The other arrests
are of Louis .Gatewood, who was
presiding over the meeting; Will
liam Douglas, acting secretary;
George H. Speed and C. M. Connor.
The last named is a member of the
Transfers union and voted for
Mayor Pratt last May.


Elizabeth Gurley Flynn and the
other prisoners will be arraigned
this afternoon before Justice Stock
ekr, to whom the cases have been
sent by Judge Mann. Bonds in the
cases has been placed at $4,000,
and Miss Flynn will be released
this afternoon on bail for $4,000 fur
nished by A. e. House and Emma
A. Stafford. The other prisoners
on the conspiracy charge today
said that they had no desire to be
released on bond, and will fight the
case out to finish.
